title: Troximon coronopifolium
intro: Troximon coronopifolium, also known as Hare's ear or Hare's tail, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, Troximon coronopifolium has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects, making it beneficial for treating wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Troximon coronopifolium include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from skin conditions to respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Troximon coronopifolium is a plant species classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the tribe Anthemideae, a subfamily of daisies and other related plants. Within the genus Troximon, it is one of several species known for their distinctive foliage and inflorescences. Troximon coronopifolium is native to Europe and Western Asia, where it can be found in a variety of habitats. As part of the Asteraceae family, it shares characteristics with other daisy-like plants that are commonly found in temperate regions.
uses: Troximon coronopifolium, commonly known as crown daisy, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
